marketFocus: NVIDIA Q4 earnings beat expectations (EPS 1.62 vs 1.54, Revenue $68.1B vs $66.12B), tech sentiment remains elevated
US-Iran nuclear talks resume in Geneva today, geopolitical tensions persist with Vance/Rubio warnings on Iran nuclear capability
BoJ hawkish dissenter Takata calls for further rate hikes, Nikkei 225 hits fresh all-time high above 59,000 before pullback
Australian Private Capex beats forecasts (0.4% vs 0.0%), feeds into next week's GDP release

USD.name: USD
USD.summary: DXY slightly softened in rangebound trade with demand constrained after weakening alongside heightened risk appetite during US trade. Fed's Musalem (2028 voter) stated inflation could stay higher for longer, although this is not his baseline, and policy is neutral now in real terms. Light data calendar today with Unemployment Claims as key focus.
drivers: DXY rangebound, weakened on risk-on sentiment and light data calendar
Fed's Musalem: inflation almost a full percentage point above target, labour market cooling orderly
Fed's Musalem: policy neutral now in real terms, balancing appropriately
Geopolitical premium: US-Iran nuclear talks in Geneva today, Vance/Rubio warn on Iran nuclear capability
Today: Unemployment Claims (exp 217K vs prev 206K)

EUR.name: EUR
EUR.summary: EUR/USD slightly firmer after returning to 1.1800 territory owing to softer buck, but with further gains limited by lack of catalysts from the bloc. European equity futures indicate slightly lower cash market open with Euro Stoxx 50 futures down 0.2%. Focus on ECB President Lagarde speech and M3 Money Supply data today.
drivers: EUR/USD returned to 1.1800 level, supported by softer DXY
European equity futures down 0.2% after Wednesday's 0.9% gains
Lack of fresh catalysts from the bloc limiting EUR upside
UK government debt sales anticipated to decline for first time in four years (GBP 247bln forecast)
Today: ECB President Lagarde speaks, M3 Money Supply y/y (exp 2.9% vs prev 2.8%)

GBP.name: GBP
GBP.summary: GBP/USD took a breather after advancing in tandem with cyclical peers. BoE's Greene stated there is a strong case for the Bank to do exactly the opposite of what the Fed is doing, and it does not make sense to set domestic policy based on another central bank. MPC Member Lombardelli speaks today.
drivers: GBP/USD consolidating after recent advances with cyclical peers
BoE's Greene: strong case to do opposite of Fed, don't set policy based on another central bank
UK government debt sales forecast to decline to GBP 247bln in approaching fiscal year
UK Foreign Secretary Cooper to announce critical minerals deal with Kazakhstan today
Today: MPC Member Lombardelli speaks, GfK Consumer Confidence (exp -15 vs prev -16)

JPY.name: JPY
JPY.summary: USD/JPY continued to pull back after climbing to best levels in over two weeks yesterday. BoJ Governor Ueda reiterated hiking bias, stating basic stance is to continue hiking rates if likelihood of forecasts materialising heightens. Hawkish dissenter Takata called for further rate hikes in gradual manner, stating BoJ should make further 'gear shift' on rates. Nikkei 225 hit fresh all-time high above 59,000 before pulling back as yen strengthened.
drivers: USD/JPY pulled back from two-week highs, yen strengthening on hawkish BoJ rhetoric
BoJ Governor Ueda: basic stance to continue hiking if likelihood of forecasts heightens
BoJ Takata (hawkish dissenter): must conduct further rate hikes in gradual manner, BoJ should make 'gear shift'
Nikkei 225 hit all-time high above 59,000 before pullback on yen strength
Today: Tokyo Core CPI y/y (exp 1.7% vs prev 2.0%), Prelim Industrial Production m/m (exp 5.5% vs prev -0.1%)

AUD.name: AUD
AUD.summary: Antipodeans kept afloat overnight following recent outperformance facilitated by high-beta status and hot Aussie CPI data from yesterday (3.8% vs 3.7%). Australian quarterly capex data topped forecasts (0.4% vs 0.0%), feeding into next week's GDP release. ASX 200 mildly gained with tech, telecoms and healthcare offsetting losses in energy and industrials.
drivers: AUD supported by hot CPI data (3.8% vs 3.7%) and better-than-expected capex (0.4% vs 0.0%)
Australian Private Capex for 2026-27 estimate AUD 158.4B, 2025-26 revised to AUD 199.3B
ASX 200 mildly gained, tech/telecoms/healthcare outperforming
High-beta status benefiting from risk-on sentiment and NVIDIA earnings beat
Today: Private Sector Credit m/m (exp 0.7% vs prev 0.8%)

CAD.name: CAD
CAD.summary: CAD pressured amid rangebound crude futures following recent choppy performance and substantial build in crude stockpiles. US-Iran nuclear talks in Geneva today keep geopolitical premium elevated. Canadian Current Account data scheduled today.
drivers: Crude futures rangebound after substantial build in crude stockpiles
US-Iran nuclear talks in Geneva today, geopolitical tensions persist
Copper futures held on to prior day's gains but with upside capped
Canadian Wholesale Sales MoM Prel (Jan) -0.6% (prev 2%)
Today: Current Account (exp -8.2B vs prev -9.7B)

CHF.name: CHF
CHF.summary: CHF mixed, benefiting from safe-haven flows amid US-Iran geopolitical tensions but pressured by EUR weakness. SNB Chairman Schlegel speaks today. Focus on GDP q/q data scheduled for Friday.
drivers: Safe-haven flows support CHF amid US-Iran nuclear talks in Geneva
EUR weakness weighs on CHF crosses
US VP Vance: see evidence Iran trying to build nuclear weapon
US Secretary of State Rubio: Iran poses grave threat, seeks nuclear capability
Today: SNB Chairman Schlegel speaks

NZD.name: NZD
NZD.summary: NZD firmer alongside AUD amid positive risk appetite and NVIDIA earnings beat. Antipodeans benefiting from broad risk-on sentiment and high-beta status. China's consulate warned Chinese citizens to avoid travel to Japan, adding to regional tensions.
drivers: NZD following AUD higher amid positive risk appetite
Risk-on sentiment: NVIDIA earnings beat (EPS 1.62 vs 1.54, Revenue $68.1B vs $66.12B)
High-beta status benefiting from tech-led gains on Wall Street
China's consulate warns citizens to avoid travel to Japan
Today: No major NZD data scheduled

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: 10yr UST futures eked slight gains, albeit with price action contained ahead of US supply, including a 7yr auction. Fed's Musalem reiterated inflation could stay higher for longer, although this is not his baseline. Little reaction to NVIDIA earnings beat.
drivers: T-note futures slightly higher ahead of 7yr auction
Fed's Musalem: inflation almost full percentage point above target
Fed's Musalem: policy neutral now in real terms, balancing appropriately
Little reaction to NVIDIA earnings beat
Today: Unemployment Claims

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bund futures struggled for direction amid recent indecision with very few fresh catalysts from the bloc. European equity futures indicate slightly lower cash market open. Focus on ECB President Lagarde speech today.
drivers: Bund futures struggled for direction, lack of fresh catalysts
European equity futures down 0.2% after Wednesday's 0.9% gains
EUR/USD returned to 1.1800 level
UK government debt sales forecast to decline to GBP 247bln
Today: ECB President Lagarde speaks

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures languished near this week's trough after yesterday's selling pressure and curve steepening on stronger growth prospects. BoJ Governor Ueda reiterated hiking bias, while hawkish dissenter Takata called for further rate hikes and stated BoJ should make 'gear shift' on rates.
drivers: JGB futures near week's trough after curve steepening
BoJ Governor Ueda: basic stance to continue hiking if likelihood of forecasts heightens
BoJ Takata: must conduct further rate hikes in gradual manner
Stronger growth prospects after Takaichi government nominated reflationists for BoJ board
Nikkei 225 hit all-time high above 59,000

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts slightly softer with UK government debt sales anticipated to decline for first time in four years. Large banks forecast GBP 247bln of gilt issuances in approaching fiscal year as Chancellor Reeves seeks to rein in borrowing.
drivers: Gilts slightly softer ahead of fiscal year debt sales forecast
UK government debt sales forecast to decline to GBP 247bln (first decline in four years)
BoE's Greene: strong case to do opposite of Fed
GBP/USD consolidating after recent advances
Today: MPC Member Lombardelli speaks
